title: U.S. Raises Steel and Aluminum Tariffs to 50%
bodyHtml:
U.S. President Donald J. Trump, through a Presidential Proclamation dated June 3, 2025, announced that the additional tariff of 25% on imported steel and aluminum products will be increased to 50%.

  • The previous 25% tariff has been raised to 50%.
  • This increase applies to both steel and aluminum products as well as their derivative articles.
  • For non-steel and non-aluminum content in the affected products, reciprocal tariffs will apply.
  • The change takes effect as of June 4, 2025.
  • Under the U.S.-UK Economic Prosperity Deal (EPD) signed on May 8, 2025, imports from the United Kingdom will remain subject to a 25% tariff. However, if the UK fails to comply with the terms of the agreement, the rate may be increased to 50%.


Click here to access the Presidential Proclamation regarding the change.
